picks.name: Dell Technologies Inc.
picks.thesis: DELL is down 12% from its 52-week high while earnings grew 34%/yr — a sentiment-driven pullback, not fundamental deterioration. Quality looks intact (healthy margins, durable revenue, long-term uptrend), and it's now at or below fair value ($415.15 vs $674.90, low confidence). Valued with DCF and Comps.

methods.name: DDM — dividend discount
methods.reason: Not meaningful: this is a growth-oriented company that reinvests and returns cash mainly via buybacks rather than dividends (yield 0.3%, payout 13%), so a dividend-only model understates it — DDM excluded

reconciliation.sentence: Only one method applied here, so treat the estimate cautiously (low confidence).

profile: ✓ → multiple compression: price down 12% while earnings grew +34%/yr (sentiment, not deterioration)
